conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who's 13 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. To determine if you might still be eligible for Child Benefit while living abroad, I need to ask:

Which country are you currently living in, and are you (or your partner if applicable) a Crown servant or working for the UK government abroad?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not a Crown servant and I don't work for the UK government. I'm just living abroad with my family.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since you're living abroad and are not a Crown servant or working for the UK government, I need to understand your circumstances a bit better.

Could you tell me which country you're currently living in?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in Spain at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since you're living in Spain, I need to understand your immigration or residency status to determine eligibility.

Do you or did you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you planning to move back to the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and I'm not planning to move back to the UK at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Result:**

I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Reason:** While Alex is 13 years old and lives with you (which would normally meet the age and responsibility requirements), the main issue is that you're living in Spain and don't meet any of the exceptions that allow people living abroad to claim Child Benefit.

To qualify for Child Benefit while living abroad, you would typically need to be:
- A Crown servant (working for the UK government abroad), OR
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with certain qualifying conditions

Since you don't meet any of these exceptions, you don't fulfill the UK residency requirement for Child Benefit.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK), you may become eligible at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old and lives with you, which would normally meet the age requirement (under 16). However, according to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. There are exceptions for people living abroad, specifically if you are a Crown servant or go to live in certain countries with specific statuses. You are living in Spain and are not a Crown servant, nor do you work for the UK government. You also do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Without meeting any of these exceptions for living abroad, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 13), who lives with them. While the child meets the age requirement, the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme). The UK residency requirement is not met, therefore they are not eligible for Child Benefit.

However, according to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. There are exceptions for people living abroad, specifically if you are a Crown servant or go to live in certain countries with specific statuses. You are living in Spain and are not a Crown servant, nor do you work for the UK government. You also do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Without meeting any of these exceptions for living abroad, you do not meet the residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 13), who lives with them. While the child meets the age requirement, the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme). The UK residency requirement is not met, therefore they are not eligible for Child Benefit.
